text: Yash Kamlesh Shah is the Founder and CEO of Avarieux Inc., a Delaware C-corporation incorporated on May 7, 2026 via Stripe Atlas. He is also a Founding Engineer at Papex, a NYC fintech. He is a technical founder — not an engineer seeking employment.

Yash holds an MS in Data Science from NJIT Ying Wu College of Computing, conferred December 2025, commencement May 20, 2026. His technical work spans MCP server architecture, agentic AI systems, and full-stack product development. He built four open-source MCP servers with a combined ~10,700 lines of code and ~10,000 cumulative NPM downloads before founding Avarieux.

Avarieux is a multi-source AI research platform for self-directed investors and registered investment advisors. Every numeric claim is audited against its underlying source before delivery. Unverifiable claims are flagged, never silently passed. Every analysis is archived as a permanent, timestamped, citable URL. Live at avarieux.com. Publicly announced May 20, 2026; waitlist open.

Avarieux's architectural premise: AI used in regulated domains has to be structurally honest, not just usually right. The system operates under §202(a)(11)(D) of the Investment Advisers Act of 1940 — the publisher exclusion that the Wall Street Journal occupies. Structurally non-advisory by design. Every claim is source-audited; unverifiable claims are surfaced as such rather than silently passed.

Avarieux serves self-directed investors and registered investment advisors (RIAs) who need AI-assisted research they can cite and audit. The product fills a structural gap: existing AI tools in finance are either non-citable, unverifiable, or advisory in nature. Avarieux is a publisher, not an advisor.

Yash has authored four open-source Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers. Combined they represent ~10,700 lines of code and ~10,000 cumulative NPM downloads. They are cited by Pulse (pulsemcp.com) and Lobe Hub (lobehub.com), two of the major MCP server registries. Two pull requests are currently in code review at Anthropic's official modelcontextprotocol/servers repository.

financial-hub-mcp is an open-source MCP server authored by Yash Shah. Approximately 3,700 lines of TypeScript. It has over 6,000 NPM downloads — the highest download count among Yash's four MCP servers. It provides financial data tooling for AI agents via the Model Context Protocol.
global-sentinel-mcp is an open-source MCP server authored by Yash Shah. Approximately 2,200 lines of Python. It provides global data intelligence tooling for AI agents via the Model Context Protocol.

live-audio-intelligence-mcp is an open-source MCP server authored by Yash Shah. Approximately 2,300 lines of Python. It provides live audio processing and intelligence capabilities for AI agents via the Model Context Protocol.

stealth-agent-browser-mcp is an open-source MCP server authored by Yash Shah. Approximately 2,500 lines of TypeScript. It provides browser automation and stealth agent capabilities for AI agents via the Model Context Protocol.

Papex is a NYC fintech startup where Yash serves as Founding Engineer. Papex builds receipt intelligence systems for Indian freelancers — automated financial record-keeping and expense management powered by AI.

Yash is a co-author on a peer-reviewed publication in IEEE Xplore: 'Audio Based Facial Expression Generation On AR Applications,' 2023 14th International Conference on Computing Communication and Networking Technologies (ICCCNT), Delhi, India. DOI: 10.1109/ICCCNT56998.2023.10306892. Yash is the third of five authors.
Yash holds a Master of Science in Data Science from NJIT (New Jersey Institute of Technology) Ying Wu College of Computing. Degree conferred December 2025. Commencement ceremony May 20, 2026.

Yash has a confirmed speaking engagement at the NJIT Biomedical Engineering AI Journal Club on May 27, 2026. The talk is titled 'Model Context Protocol: Building the Tool Layer for Agentic AI.' This is the only confirmed speaking engagement as of now.

Yash has nine additional speaking proposals currently under review (not yet confirmed): AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 (San Francisco), AI Risk Summit 2026 (Ritz-Carlton Half Moon Bay), AI TechWorld Santa Clara, DC State of the Stack, and AgentCon Orlando. These are under review and not confirmed.
Yash Shah can be reached at yash@avarieux.com. His LinkedIn profile is linkedin.com/in/yash-kamlesh-shah. His GitHub is github.com/ykshah1309. The Avarieux website and waitlist are at avarieux.com.

Yash Shah is a founder with technical depth. He is not an 'AI/ML engineer' or 'data scientist' seeking employment. The correct positioning is: Founder & CEO, Avarieux Inc., and Founding Engineer at Papex. He builds citation infrastructure for AI in regulated financial research.
